title: rexnzm/MCP-rag-with-Chromadb
summary: 🗂️ Build a versatile MCP server with RAG capabilities, supporting multiple document formats using LangChain and ChromaDB for efficient data retrieval.

title: DndMuzik/Agentic-AI-Trip-Planner-CrewAI
summary: 🌍 Create personalized travel itineraries with Agentic AI Trip Planner, leveraging CrewAI for real-time search and intelligent trip planning.

title: IamNotShady/LLMIngress
summary: Self-hosted AI gateway for agents — route providers, virtual models, limits, and usage from one console.

title: javieroon/Multi-Backend-Chatbot-with-Gradio
summary: 🤖 Build a multi-backend chatbot with Gradio, integrating 5 AI providers and over 100 free models for versatile and user-friendly interactions.

title: OpenAgentHQ/modeldock
summary: The lightweight, Python-first model manager for local LLMs - the package manager for local AI models. Discover, download, cache, verify, and load models through pluggable runtime adapters (Ollama, LM Studio, llama.cpp, and more). 100+ good-first-issues welcome!

title: gafaar22/recursive-prompt-improver
summary: 🛠 Enhance LLM prompts effortlessly with RPI, a tool for testing, improving, and managing prompts across various AI providers.

title: XAIHT/Tlamatini
summary: Tlamatini is a self-hosted AI developer assistant for local or cloud LLMs: hybrid RAG over your codebase, a 79-tool multi-turn engine, a 74-agent visual workflow designer, and ACPX, a runtime for external coding agents like Claude Code, Cursor and Gemini CLI.

title: piporyx/doc-qa-agent
summary: 🤖 Create an interactive chatbot that answers questions from documents using OpenAI and Ollama, with a user-friendly Gradio interface.

title: mmontes11/k8s-ai
summary: 🧠 Tenant repository bootstrapped by k8s-infrastructure that contains the manifests for AI related applications

title: pdbethke/corralai
summary: One directive → a herd of AI agents that plan, build, verify, and re-plan across any model (local 7B to frontier) — human-gated, jail→repo, with shared memory + skills. Every mission recorded and replayable.

title: bedeberger/schreibwerkstatt
summary: KI-gestütztes Lektorat-Tool für BookStack

title: WynandJvR/Animus
summary: AI-controllable Minecraft bot with a swappable brain - drive it with a local Ollama model or Claude over an HTTP API. Gathers, crafts, and builds schematics autonomously in survival.

title: attenlabs/hotato
summary: Regression testing for voice agents. The transcript passed; the call failed. hotato scores the turn timing and checks what the agent said against what the backend did, then gates CI. Self-hosted, MIT.

title: PatriceA/octipus
summary: An open-source, self-hosted assistant that delegates real work to a swarm of specialist AI agents.

title: richhabits/sam
summary: A free, private, local-first AI assistant that actually does the work — 167 tools, a team of 78 AI agents, 40 rotating free AI brains, 25 skills. Not a chatbot, a doer. It doesn't just answer — it handles it.

title: pd95/local-agent-container
summary: Local agent container on your Mac

title: psancheti6666/sotto
summary: Private, local dictation for macOS & Linux — hold a key, speak, and clean punctuated text lands at your cursor. 100% offline: on-device speech recognition + LLM cleanup, $0 to run.

title: mnimtz/printix-tonerwatch
summary: Multi-tenant Printix BI toner monitor + ordering console for MSPs. Autonomous ordering with AI SKU suggestions (OpenAI/Azure/Gemini/Anthropic/Ollama) + daily cap. Alerts via Resend/SMTP/Graph. Entra SSO auto-setup, Azure SQL/SQLite, Azure Blob backup, M365 Copilot indexing. Kanban, saved views, activity feed. EFIGS i18n.

title: Nile6335/civiclens
summary: Multimodal RAG over city-council records — meeting videos, agenda PDFs, and budget tables — with a multi-agent pipeline, hybrid retrieval, and a CI-gated evaluation harness. Local-first: no API keys required.

title: elemsi/forecastgpt-financial-outlook-agent
summary: 📊 Automate financial analysis and forecasting with ForecastGPT, leveraging AI to extract insights from reports and streamline decision-making.
